title: Glacial-interglacial trench supply variation, spreading-ridge subduction, and feedback controls on the Andean margin development at the Chile triple junction area (45–48°S)
journal: Journal of Geophysical Research: Solid Earth
abstract: EM12 bathymetry and seismic reflection data were collected during the Chile Triple Junction cruise (March–April 1997) offshore 45–48°S, where an active spreading ridge is being subducted beneath the Andean continental margin. The Andean continental margin and Chile trench show strong segmentation reflecting the slab geometry and CTJ migration history. The study reveals a margin development shaped by processes spanning from subduction-erosion to subduction-accretion.

processing.summary: Seismic reflection profiles and EM12 multibeam bathymetry collected during the 1997 Chile Triple Junction cruise were processed and interpreted to document margin development at 45°–48°S. The profiles image the accretionary prism, trench fill, and margin segmentation associated with the Chile Ridge subduction. Glacial-interglacial variations in trench sediment supply are reconstructed from seismic stratigraphy.
workflow: 4. Seismic interpretation and correlation with bathymetry
